task: Direct, immediate follow-up on the just-shipped full-screen Gate: "ok i like the full screen thing but rest is not working out. remove the environment elements." The old illustrative ambient-sky decoration layer -- .gateway-scene's clouds/islands/birds/particles/rays/haze/sun/wind-streaks, plus Scene 5's separate .gateway-journey-extras streaming cloud/mountain/bird parallax bands -- only ever clashed with a real, full-bleed cinematic Gate video: they'd bleed through visibly during the Gate's own 1.8s opacity fade-in (confirmed directly -- a mobile screenshot taken 400ms after mount showed real cloud/bird shapes overlaid on the doors), and even once the Gate turned fully opaque, the whole layer was pure dead weight sitting behind it. Removed entirely: index.html's .gateway-scene div (17 decorative child elements) is gone outright, leaving only #gatewayContent inside #gatewayOverlay -- .gateway-overlay's own background gradient is the sole remaining pre-video atmosphere, invisible the instant the Gate paints. css/style.css lost the full .gateway-scene-*/.gateway-wind-streak rule set (~150 lines: sky/haze/rays/sun gradients, 3 clouds with pseudo-element puffs, 3 islands, 3 birds, 6 particles, 3 wind streaks, and their 6 keyframes) plus the Journey's own .gateway-journey-extras/-near/-far parallax bands and 2 keyframes, plus every reduced-motion and .gateway-hush rule that referenced the now-removed selectors (the halo-dimming half of .gateway-hush is kept, since the Gate's own glow effect is unrelated and unaffected). js/gatewaySequence.js's playApproachLeadIn() and playJourney() are simplified to match -- the wind-rising/journey-active class toggles and the emoji-span construction in playJourney() are removed since they drove zero remaining CSS, while the underlying WIND_RISE_MS/JOURNEY_MS timing beats themselves (and their place in the six-scene pipeline) are kept unchanged, so overall pacing is unaffected. Verified via a full re-run of the existing 41-assertion Gateway Playwright suite (21 Traveller-path + 7 Returning-Creator-path + 13 full-screen-coverage geometry assertions, all unchanged since none of them ever depended on the removed decorations) plus a direct before/after mobile screenshot comparison confirming the exact cloud/bird bleed-through artifact is gone -- the Gate now reads as a clean, uncluttered cinematic backdrop from the very first frame at every viewport size. Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected) pass unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to index.html, css/style.css, and js/gatewaySequence.js, with a matching cache-bust version bump on both stylesheet <link> tags.
